title: Official Documents- Financing Agreement for Credit No. 6675-BD
text:
﻿CREDIT NUMBER 6675-BD
Financing Agreement
(Enhancing Digital Government and Econony Project)
between
PEOPLE'S REPUBIIC OF BANGLADESII
INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT ASSOCIATION


CREDIT NUMBER 6675-BD
FINANCING AGREEMENT
AGREEMENT dated as of the Signature Date between PEOPLES REPUBLIC
OF   BANGI ADESH1 (Recipient") and      INTERNATIONAL      DEVELOPMENT
ASSOCIATION (Association'). The Recipient and the Association hereby agree as
follows:
ARTICLE I - GENERAL CONDITIONS; DEFINITIONS
1.01,  The General Conditions las defined in the Appendix to this Agreement) apply to
and form part of this Agreement
I 02.  Unless the context requires otherwise. the capitalized terms used in this Agreement
have the meaninus ascribed to them in the General Conditions or in the Appendix
to this Agrcement.
ARTICLE II- CREDIT
2 01.   The Association agrees to extend to the Recipient a credit, which is deemed as
Non-concessional Financing for purposes of the General Conditions, in the amount
of two hundred seventy-one million and three hundred thousand Euro
i EUR 271.300.000). as such amount may be converted from time to time through
a CuTencV Conversion ("Credit"), to assist in financing the project described in
Schedule I to this Agreement ('Project").
2.02.  The Recipient may withdraw the proceeds of the Credit in accordance with Section
Ill of Schedule 2 to this Agreement.
2.03.  The Front-end Fee is one quarter of one percent (' of 1%) of the Credit amount.
2.04.  The Commitment Charge is one-quarter of one percent 1 of 1 %) per annum on
the Unwithdrawn Credit Balance.
2.05.  The Interest Charue is the Reference Rate plus the Fixed Spread or such rate as
may apply following a Conversion, subject to Section 3.09(e) of the General
Conditions.
2.16.  The Payment Dates are May I and November I in each year.


2.07.  The principal amount of the Credit shall be repaid in accordance with Schedule 3
to this Agreement.
ARTICLE III - PROJECT
3101   The Recipient declares its commitment to the objective of the Project. To this end.
the Recipient shall carry out the Project, through BCC. in accordance with the
provisions of Article V of the General Conditions and Schedule 2 to this
Agreement.
ARTICLE IV - EFFECTIVENESS: TERMINATION
4.01. The Fffecticeness Deadline is the date ninety 190) days after the date of this
Signature Date.
ARTICLE V- REPRESENTATIVE; ADDRESSES
5101. The Recipient's Representative is the Senior Secretary,/Secretary. any Additional
Secretary, the Joint SecretarvJoint Chief, the Deputy SecretarvDeputy Chief. the
Senior  Assistant Secretarv;Senior  Assistant Chief  or  the  Assistant
Secretary Assistant Chief of the Economic Relations Division of the Recipient's
Ministry of Finance.
5,02  For purposes of Section 10(01 of the General Conditions:
ta) the Recipient's address is:
Economic Relations Di\ ision

Dhaka 1207
Banu ladesh

1818 H Street, NW«

(b) the ANssociation's Electronic Address is:
Tele:               Feacsinile:

SCHEDULE 1
Project Desmiption
The obective of the Proct is to improve the efficiency and cybersecuritv of
digital government. and inerease Digitally Enabled Emplovnient and raise revenues of IT
industry firms supported by the Projeet.
The Project consists of the followving parts:
Part 1: Enabling Environment for Digital Government and Digital Econoni
1.1.   Leual and Regiulatorv Enabling lnvironient for Digutal Government and the
Diial Econony
(i)    Support for review. development and implernentation of the policy. legal
and regxilatory frameworks. and strengthening institutional and data
governance arrangements needed to support digital government and the
transition to a digital economy,
ii)    Establishment and strengthcning of data protection satguards. including
institutions responsible for data protection, to strengthen data privacy and
prevent the misuse of data.
(iii)  Support for BCC for implementation of organization ehanges proposed
under sub-parts (i) and (ii) abov e.
ix )   Development and implementation of business plan for integrated digital
plattori to ensure its technical operational and financial sustainabilitv
and climale resilience,
1.2.  Change and Stakeholder Managmenit
Development and implementation of a change and stakeholder management
program for selected Go13 agencies. ihrough. wter aha:
ii)    assessing the impact of digital government on relevant public sector jobs:
(ii)   conducting stakeholder and political ceonony analysis of suich GoB
auencies and their staff, and designing measures to mitigate potential
negati\ e impaets on civ il serv ants including training. redeplovment, and
mentorship for affected personnel;


-6-
(iii)  conducting necessary staff engagements. sensitization workshops and
trainings for civil servants to adapt to digital vov ernment-related changes:
and
(i)    conducting process reengineering to facilitate institutional coordination
between such (oB agencies and BCC.
13.    GoB Divital Capacity De\elopment
(i)    Establishment of a digital leadership academy under BCC as a center of
excellence for knowledge or disruptive!energing technologies, including:
(a)    setting up the digital leadership academy.
(b)    establishing partnerships wvith leading global institutes in digital
go\ ernment and econorny to bring in global expertise on emerging
technologies, apply international best practices for the Project's
implementation, and provide specialized training for selected
govemnent officials:
(c)    providing assistance to GoB agencies with business continuity
planning for pandemics and other crises; and
(d)    providing support to selected Public Universities for providing
digital skills training to GoB officials.
(ii)   Establishment of a digital intellectual property agency uider BCC as a
center of excellence for dicital-related intellectual property issues and
activities, including:
(a)    setting up the digital intellectual property agency by rehabilitating
existing government office space:
(b)    formulating intellectual property best practices, standards and
policies for the digital innovation- and
(c)    providing advisory and guidance at the national level on digital
intellectual property,
Part 2: Transforming Digital Government
11. uInerated DiwHal Platform for Diwital Go%crnmcnt and Diuital Economy
Establishment of an integrated digital platform as a shared, cloud-computing
platform for use by any GoB agency to build. operate and maintain its sector-specific
systems. applications and scr ices. including the proxision of:


(a)    infrastructure-as-a-service to cloud-enable the National Data
Center:
(b)    platform-as-a-service to develop a shared IT platform for GoB
agencies to build, operate and maintain their applications and or
public services: and
(c)    software-as-a-ser\ ice to provide common applications for GoB
employees. including document management system, electronic
cataloLuc-based procurement. go\ernment resource planning and
online communications and collaboration tools
2.2.  CvberseCuritv sirenuthening for GoB
Scaling up of BCC cybersecurity activities to enhance GoB internal network
sCcurity. specifically.
(i)    BCC assessing cvbersecurity gaps in selected GoB agencies:
(ii)   upgrading BCC*s cvber defense training center, computer incidence
response team laboratory, and forensic laboratory:
(iii)  BCC installing basic end-point cybersecurity package for various GoB
ministries and div isions:
(iv) BCC installing cybersensors for govenument districts and upazillas for
monitoring internal GoB network security:
(v)   BCC providing email security for GoB email accounts; and
(i)   BCC pro\ iding secure web gateways and/or unidirectional gateways for
key GoB ministries.
2.3   Mainstreamin Bantladesh National Diital Architecture (BNDA)
(i)    Institutionalization of BNDA as the architectural blueprint and the ie fiwto
guideline for implementation of ICT" initiatives across GoB.
(ii)   Preparation of implementation plan and roadrnap for interoperabilitv
between systems.
(iii)  Sensitization and promotion of BNDA framework among GoB ministries
and divisions


Part 3: Developing Digital Enablers
1 1.  Diiltal Enablers Coordination
Establishment of a Center of Excellence on Digital Technologies within BCC,
which Center shall:
(a)    update the ICT Di ision*s digital economy strategy with relevant
policy recommendations based on best international practices, and
communicate it across GoB agencies. industry and academia:
(b)    assist the ICT Dix ision to coordinate its programs and projects on
IT industry development:
(c)    assist GoB with technical assessment of its digital economy-
related policies;
(d)    conduct surey to find gaps in women's participation in the ICT
sector; and
(c)    develop and provide sector transformation road maps for strategic
sectors, such as healthcare, mainuIFIcturing, education and
agriculture, including in the wake of crisis such as COVID-19 and
climate change.
3, 2  lire and Train Prograni for Youth
Scaling up of GoB's ongoing hire and train program to proxide training to new hires
in Selected Companies on emerging/disruptive technologV or technology that
supports development of the digital economy through support to selected Public
Universities to conduct said training.
33.   Strenuthening and Promoting tile IT Inclustry
(i)    Scaling up of Gobs program for promoting local IT frns in international
markets and assisting them in establishing partnerships with global IT
companies andor acquiring international clients, Including developing an
international business dev elopment plan: building a contacts database of
relevant target clients and partners- proposing and assisting in the setup of
two business development hubs in relevant countries to implement the
industry promotion plan: organizing and implementing external and
internal business dc\ elopment trips lor local IT firms to meet with targeted
executives in international markets: and providing business development
and sales support and services to local firms that are engaging with
international clients.


-9-
(ii)  (a) Establishment of two hubs for the ICT industry with two relevant
industry associations. for [he purpose of" inter aha, producing use-case
demos and showcasing made-in-Bangladesh IT solutions: identifying and
implementing upskilling and reskilling opportunities within the IT
industry: coordinating with the Center of Excellence on Digital
Technologies to foster a community of local digital inno\ators: (b)
Facilitation of the use of the hubs by companies to familiarize them with
ICT productivity tools; and (c) Conducting of trials and pilots for emerging
technologies by local entrepreneurs and firms.
3.4.  Diuitization of Small and Medium Enterprises
i)    Implementation of a strategic partnership program to help local small and
medium enterprises scale up and,or level their digital capabilities and
expand  their global reach  through  collaborations with  global
organ izat ions
iii)   Formulation and provision of a comprehensive set of assistance schemes
for small and medium enterprises to support their digitization.
1iii)  De\ clopmcnt of a digital business continuity plan for small and medium
enterprises to mitigate or maintain core business functions and enable
quick restarts in the event of a maJor disruption.
3.    Establish Trainini, Research and Innox anon Centers
(i)   Establishment of training, research and innovation centers within selected
Public Universities to improve digital skills of students, develop emerging
technology strategies and plans for research and innovation. and build
research and innovation capacities, including through the creation of
technical partnerships.
iii)  Provision of Fees to teachers from selected Public Universities for research
and innovation initiaties,
iii)  Support to selected Public Universities to pro\ ide digital skills training to
students from relevant disciplines.
Part 4: Project Management Support
4. I.  Set-up, Operations and Auditing of Project Implementation Unit
Establishment of a Protect Implementation Unit (PIU) for Project implementation
support. including planning, coordination, implementation, and monitoring of
Project activities: reporting progress on climate change adaptation and mitigation:
procurement and financial management: and overall reporting on Project progress.


-lot-
4.2.   Poe M             d anaementadipleSmentation Support
Support for PILU for o\ erall Projcct managemeni and implemeintation, including
through project management and iniplernentation technical support.


-1I-
SCHEDULE 2
Project Execution
Section 1.   Implementation Arrangements
A. Institutional Arrangements
1.     The Recipient shall establish within three (3) months of the Effective Date, and
thereafter maintain throuhout the course of Project implementation. a Project
steering committee (PSC) with a mandate. functions, composition and resources.
satisfactorv to the Association. Without limitation on the foregoim!. said
committee shall: (a) be chaired by the secretary of the ICT Division and include
representatives from  the relevant ministries agencies involved in Project
implementation: and (b) meet at least once in every three (3) months. and (c) be
responsible for, inter alia, reviewing the reconunendation of the Project
implementation committee referred to in paragraph 2 below for addressing
problems that arise during Project implementation and to take decision
accordingly: giving guideline or formulating policies required for implementing
Project activities; and carrvingi- out any other matters related to Project
implementation.
1      The Recipient shall establish within three (3) months of' tie Effective Date, and
thereafter maintain throughout the course of Project implementation, a Project
implementation comlifittee (PIC) with a mandate. functions and resources.
satisfactory to the Association, and with staff in adequate numbers and with
qualifications, experience and terms of reference satisfactory to the Association,
Without limitation on the foregoin,. said office shall. (a) be headed by the
executive director of BCC   and  include representatives from  relevant
ministries auencies involved in Project implementation: (b) meet at least once in
every three (3) months; and (c) be responsible for, inter alia. giving necessary
assistance or suggestion for implementing Project activities; and giving necessary
decision to solve problems arising during Project implementation.
3.     The Recipient shall establish within one 1) nonth of the Effective Date, and
thereafter maintain throughout the course of ProJect implementation. a Project
Implementation Unit (PlL) w%ith a mandate. functions and resources satisfactorv to
the Association. and with staff in adequate numbers and wvith qualifications.
experience and terms of reference satisfactory to the Association. Without
limitation on the foregoing, said office shall: (a) be headed by a Project director
and a deputy Project director. and (b) be responsible for, inter aba: (i) planning,
coordination, implementation and monitoring and evaluation of Project activities:
(ii) procurement and financial management: and (iii) reporting on Project progress.


-12-
B.     Project Operations Manual
I     The Recipient shall prepare, appro\e and adopt a Project Operations Manual, in a
maier satisfhctorv to the Association, setting forth the specific rules and
procedures for implementing the Project, including. inter al/a: (a) detailed
description of Project actiities: (b) results framework: (c) overall budget: (d)
implementation arrangements for Parts 1. 3 and 4 of the Project. (c) modalities for
providing training under Paris I.3(i)(d). 3.2 and 3.5(1iii) of the Project. including
eliuibility and selection criteria and selection process for Public Uni\ersities and
Selected Companies, monitoring and reporting arrangements and templates for
MoUs: (f) modalities for pro\ iding Fees to Beneficiaries. including eligibility and
selection criteria and selection process. and monitoring and reporting
arrangements: (g) arrangements for funds flow, accountinL, internal control.
reporting and oversight: and (h) Project's safeguards requirements.
2.     Except as the Association shall otherwise agree in writg, the Recipient shall not
assign, amend, abrogate, or waive. or permit to be assigned, amended, abrogated,
or waived. said manual andor any provision thereof.
3, In the eent ofany inconsistency between the provisions of the Project Operations
Manual and those of this Agreement. this Agreement shall govern.
C.     Digital Government Operations Manual
1.     The Recipient shall prepare, approve and adopt a Digital Go\errnent Operations
Manual. in a mnner satisfactory to the Association, setting forth the
implementation arrangements, uiidelines and standards to be used for
implementation of Part 2 of the Project, designed to ensure, inter alia, that the
Integrated digital platform and BNDA are implemented on data protection and
indi\idual privacy. including principles of proportionality and minimal data
collection and disclosure, user control. network security and access manacement,
and institutional and governance arrangements, guided by international best
practices and consistent with the Principles on Identi fication for Sustainable
Development. and preventing \endor lock-in for new procurements. and ensure
interoperability and common standards for digital govcrnment systems and
infrastructure: and establishing institutional responsibilities, mandates and
accountability for the purposes of Part 2 of the Project.
2      Except as the Association shall otherwN ise agree in writing, the Recipient shall not
assiLn, amend, abrogate, or waive, or permit to be assigned, amended, abrogated,
or waived, said manual and-or an\ pro\ ision thereof.
3      In the event of any inconsistency between the provisions of the Digital
Go\ernmient Operations Manual and those of this Agreement, this Avrecment shall
go\ ern.


D.     Safeguards
I     The Recipient shall ensure that the Project is carried out with due regard to
appropriate health. safety, social, and environmental standards and practices. and
in accordance with the Safecuards Instruments.
1      The Recipient shall ensure that the obligation to comply with the relevant
Safeguard Instruments is incorporated: a in the contracts between the Recipient
and the relevant contractor(s) and any entity (including any engineer) super\ ising
the Project's ci\ il works: and (b) in the contracts between the relevant contractorts)
and the contractors' subcontractors.
3.      The Borrower shall ensure that all the bidding documents and contracts include the
obligation of the relevant contractors and subcontractors to (i) adopt and
implement meastUres to assess and manage the risks and impacts of labor influx
and workers' camps: and (ii) adopt and enforce codes of conduct that should be
provided to and signed by all workers; as applicable to such civil works
commissioned or carried out pursuant to said contracts
4.     The Recipient shall ensure that the Project does not include any activities and
expenditures on the negative list set forth in the ESMF
5      Except as the Association shall otherwise agree, the Recipient shall ensure, and
cause to ensure. that none of the provisions of the Safeguard Instruments is
abrogated, amended, repealed. suspended or waived. In case of any inconsistencies
betw,veen the provisions of any of the Safeguard Instruments and the pro\ isions of'
this Agreement, the pro\ isions of this Agreement shall pre\ail
E.     Provision of Support to Public Vniversities
1.     The Recipient shall. through BCC, invite and competitively select IT companies
"Selected Companics") for receiving training support under Part  of the
Protect. in accordance with the selection criteria and procedures set forth in the
Project Operations Manual.
2.     For purposes of carrying out the acti ities under Parts 1.3(o)fd), 32 and 35(iii) of
the Project, the Recipient shall. through BCC. invite Public Universities to submit
proposals: and screen and select the proposals in accordance with the selection
criteria and procedures set forth in the Project Operations ManuaL including. inter
abla:
(a)    that the proposals of selected Public I niversities are approved by
BCC in a manner satisfactory to the Association: and


-14-
(b)    that each selected Public Universitv enters into a memorandum of
understanding (MoU) with the BCC under terms satisfactory to
the Association, as set fbrth in the Project Operations Manual, and
pursuant to the respective template MoU prescribed in the Project
Operations Manual, which terms include, inter ala
(i)    the requirement that the selected Public University shall
carry out acti\ ities financed under the Project with due
diligence and efficiency and in accordance with sound
technical. financial. en ironmental and managerial
standards, including the provisions of the Project
Operations Manual, the Procurement Regulations. the
Anti-Corruption  Guidelines  and  the  Safeguard
Instrunients:
ii)   the requirement that the selected Public UniversitV shall:
(A) establish and/or maintain policies and procedures that
would allow the Recipient and!or the Association to carry
out supervision and monitor the implementation of its
activitics under the Project. (B) prepare and furnish to the
Recipient and,or the Association, all such information that
the Recipient and/or the Association shall reasonably
request in relation to such acti\ ities: and (C) accept random
and or unannotmnced physical or documentary inspections
hy the Recipient andior the Association for the monitoring
of, and in relation to, the carrying out of such activities:
iii)  the right of the Recipient to stispend or terminate the right
of the selected Public University to receive any support
under the Project: (A) upon any failure of the selected
Public Universitv to perform it obligations nder the
MoU; or (B) upon the Association declaring the selected
PUblic Unikersity neliiible under the Anti-Corruption
Guidelines.
3.     The Recipient shall exercise its rights tinder the MoU in such manner as to protect
the interests of the Recipient and the Association and to accomplish the purposes
of the Financing. Except as the Association shall otherwise agree, the Recipient
shall not assign, amend, abrogate or waive the MoU or any of its provisions.
F.     Fees to Public University Teachers
I For the purpose of carrying otit activities under Part 3.5(ii of the Project, the
Recipient shall, through BCC. competitikely select teachers ("Beneficiaries")
from selected Public Universities to receive Fees for research and innovation


-I-
initiaties. in accordance with the eligibility and selection criteria and
procedures and transaction mechanisms and using appropriate documents,
acceptable to the Association and set forth in the Project Operations Manual,
such mechanisms to include, inter a/ia:
(a)    for payment of the Fees for the first time. confirmation that the Beneficiary
has started the proposed research and innovation initiaie. such
confirmation to be made in accordance with the terms set forth in the
Project Operations Manual:
(b)    for continued payment of Fees. confirmation that the Beneficiary has been
making satisfactory progress in the proposed research and innovation
initiative, such confirmation to be made in accordance with the terms set
forth in the Project Operations Manual: and
Ic)    the right of the Recipient to suspend or terminate the provision of Fees to
any Beneficiary upon any failure of such Beneficiary to perform herhis
obligations in accordance with the Project Operations Manual.
.         The Recipient shall ensure that the Bcneficiary shall:
Ia)    establish and'or maintain policies and procedures that would allow the
Recipient andor the Association to carry out super\ ision and monitoring
of the implementation of the Beneficiary's activities under the Project,
(b)    prepare and furnish to the Recipient and"or the Association. all such
information that the Recipient and:or the Association may reasonably
request in relation to the Beneficiary's acti\ ities under the Project: and
Ic)    accept random andor unannounced physical or documentary Inspections
by the Recipient andor the Association for the monitoring of. and in
relation to, the carrvng out of the Beneficiary's actikities under the
Project
G.     Data Protection and Privacy
The Recipient. through BCC. shall cause an independent report by legal and technical
expert with recipient country knowledge and experience, under terms of reference
satisfactory to the Association, to be prepared and delivered to the ICT Division for
transmission to Minister of ICT. for review, endorsement and publication, within
twenty-four (24) months of the Effective Date. Such report shall include an analysis of
the policy, legal and rCgulatory frane\ork relating to data protection, and data pri\acy
and reform. havinig regard to principles of proportionality and minimal data collection
and disclosure, user control, network securit\ and access management. and
institutional and governance arrangements, guLided by international good practices,


-16-
including the Principles on Identification for Sustainable Development, and make
recommendations for policy. legal and,or regulatory reforms based on such analsis.
H1.    Expenditures to be Exclusively Financed with Counterpart Funds
The Recipient shall: (a) ensure that the following expenditures are financed
exclusively out of its own resources and not out of the proceeds of the Financing:
and (b) provide, promptly as needed, the resources needed for this purpose:
0i)    all costs associated with land and land use rights required for the purposes
of the Project.
(ii)   all resettlement and rehabilitation compensation and other assistance to
Affected Persons, in each case in accordance NN ith the RPF and any plan(s)
prepared or to be prepared thereunder:
Iiii)  procurement of vehicles. recurrent expenditures for the purpose of
attending meetings. conferences, seminars. workshops and study visits
(sitting allowances / cash per diems   honoraria, notwithstanding eligible
expenditures under incremental Operating Costs and Training), and
recurrent expenditures for fuel, under the Project; and
(i)    taxes exceeding fifiecen (15) percent of the total amount of Financing.
Section II.   Proiect Monitoring. Reporting and Evaluation
The Recipient shall furnish to the Association each Project Report not later than
forty-five (4i) days after the end of each calendar semester, coverin the calendar
semester.
Section III.  Withdraval of the Proceeds of the Credit
A.     General
Without limitation upon the provisions of Article 11 of the General Conditions and
in accordance with the Disbursement and Financial Information Letter, the
Recipient may withdraw the proceeds of the Credit to: (a) finance Eligible
Expenditures: and (b) pay: each Interest Rate Cap or Interest Rate ('ollar premium.
in the amount allocated and. if applicable. up to the percentage set forth against
each Category in the following table:

B.      Withdrawal Conditions. Withdrawal Period
I.     Notwithstanding the provisions of Part A above, no withdrawal shall be made:


-18-

(b)    under Categorv 2, until and unless the Recipient has prepared, approved
and adopted a Digital Government Operations Manual satisfactory to the
Association: or
(c)    under Category 3 or 4. until and unless the Recipient has prepared.

Association
2.     The Closing Date is September 30. 2025.

APPENDIX
Definitions
.      A"AiTeeted Person" means a person or entity who. on account of the e\ecution of
the Project, has experienced or would experience direct cconomic and social
impaets caused by: (i) the involuntary taking of land resulting in: (a) relocation or
loss of shelter. (b) loss of assets or access to assets: or (e) loss of inconic sources
or means of' l,ivelihood, whether or not such person must move to another location:
or (ii) the involuntary restriction of access to legallv designated parks and protected
areas, resulting in adverse impacis on the lihlihood of such person.
2.     "Ant-Corruption Goideines" means. for purposes of paragraph 5 of the Appendix
to the General Conditions. the "Guidelines on Preventing and Combating Fraud
and Corruption in Projects Financed by IBRD Loans and IDA Credits and Grants",
dated October 15. 2006 and revised in Januarv 20 1 and as of July 1. 20l6.
3.     "Baniladesh National Digital Architecture" or "BNDA" means the Recipient's
national IT system architecture, to support the management and evolution of IT
svstens, and business operations, of Go13, which architecture coiprises of the
following five architctural components: organizati onal arehitcettire. business
